categories.title: Deployment & Infrastructure
categories.description: Railway migration, marketing site, CI/CD, monitoring

tasks.title: Apply session-tracking DB migration (UserSession, UserMetrics, UserDeletionLog)
instructions: Open Supabase SQL Editor: https://supabase.com/dashboard/project/oaogpsshewmcazhehryl/sql/new
Open the migration file in your editor: prisma/migrations/20260413200000_add_user_session_tracking/migration.sql
Copy the ENTIRE contents (all 95 lines)
Paste into the Supabase SQL editor text area
Click the green 'Run' button in the top-right (or Cmd+Enter / Ctrl+Enter)
Expect 'Success. No rows returned' in green at the bottom — this is correct for CREATE TABLE
Verify: paste `SELECT table_name FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema = 'public' AND table_name IN ('UserSession', 'UserMetrics', 'UserDeletionLog');` and Run — should see 3 rows
Rollback if anything goes wrong: DROP TABLE "UserSession", "UserMetrics", "UserDeletionLog" CASCADE;
Once verified, mark this task done and tell Claude 'migration applied'
tasks.notes: One-time step. Creates 3 new tables — touches nothing existing. Safe; fully reversible via DROP TABLE CASCADE.

tasks.title: Set IP_HASH_SECRET env var in Railway
instructions: Generate a fresh secret locally: openssl rand -hex 32
Open Railway dashboard: https://railway.app/dashboard
Click your FBST API service
Click the 'Variables' tab
Add or update IP_HASH_SECRET with the generated value — NEVER paste a real secret into this file
Click Save — Railway will trigger a redeploy automatically (~2 min)
Verify deploy succeeded in the 'Deployments' tab
tasks.notes: 32-byte hex secret used by lib/ipHash.ts to HMAC user IPs + emails (UserDeletionLog.hashedEmail). Fail-fast at server boot if missing. Rotate yearly per plan R8. Rotated 2026-04-16 during Session 65 commit; file redacted in same commit.

tasks.title: Stripe Checkout flow for Pro/Commissioner
instructions: Install @stripe/stripe-js and stripe (server) packages
Create server/src/features/billing/routes.ts with POST /api/billing/checkout (creates Stripe Checkout Session)
Add POST /api/stripe/webhook to process checkout.session.completed + subscription lifecycle events
Update UserProfile schema with plan (free/pro/commissioner) and stripeCustomerId
Wire 'Upgrade' button on /pricing to Checkout
Add success/cancel return URLs (/pricing?success=1)

tasks.title: Stripe subscription lifecycle management
instructions: Handle subscription lifecycle: create, renew, cancel, pause
Webhook handlers for invoice.paid, invoice.payment_failed, customer.subscription.deleted
Grace period for failed payments (7 days before feature lockout)
Store subscription status on User model

tasks.title: Feature gating based on plan (free/pro/commissioner)
instructions: Create requirePlan(plan) middleware checking req.user.plan
Gate AI features (Weekly Insights, Draft Report, trade/waiver analysis) behind 'pro'
Gate Commissioner tools (auction admin, force-assign) behind 'commissioner'
Show upgrade prompt on gated features in UI
Keep all other features free (drafts, standings, trades, waivers)

categories.title: Session 63 /ce:review Backlog
categories.description: P1/P2 findings from the 7-agent code review synthesized after Session 63. See docs/plans/2026-04-13-admin-dashboard-api-contract.md and FEEDBACK.md Session 64 entry for context.

tasks.title: Rewrite heartbeat as single conditional UPDATE (drop SELECT+UPDATE race)
instructions: File: server/src/features/sessions/routes.ts
Current: SELECT to check session existence, then UPDATE lastSeenAt
Target: single UPDATE ... WHERE id = ? AND userId = ? (use affected-row count to infer existence)
Cuts DB round-trips in half and closes a subtle dedupe race where two heartbeats could both pass the SELECT and double-write
Verify with existing sessions tests; add one regression test for the race window
tasks.notes: Flagged by performance-oracle + data-integrity-guardian in Session 63 /ce:review. Not yet landed.

tasks.title: Replace /admin/stats active30d raw SQL with UserMetrics.lastLoginAt query
instructions: File: server/src/features/admin/routes.ts (computeAdminStats / active30d)
Current: raw SQL against AuditLog scanning for LOGIN events in last 30d — caused MaxClientsInSessionMode pooler exhaustion today
Target: `prisma.userMetrics.count({ where: { lastLoginAt: { gte: thirtyDaysAgo } } })` — index-backed
Verify UserMetrics is written on every login (see plan R11 upsert — check post-session-tracking rollout)
Benchmark before/after on prod dataset
tasks.notes: Urgent. Supabase pooler saw connection pressure in Session 64 dev. This query is the suspected culprit.

tasks.title: /admin/stats single-flight dedup (N dashboards on cache expiry = N full query sets)
instructions: File: server/src/features/admin/routes.ts (statsCache logic around line 692)
Current: TTL cache only — on expiry, N concurrent requests each run the full query set
Target: single-flight pattern — first request on cache miss computes, others await the same promise
Simple impl: track an `inflightPromise` alongside `statsCache`; null it on completion
Add concurrency test (fire 10 parallel requests, assert only one DB call)

tasks.title: Fix /admin/stats metric bugs (leaguesOwned returns team count; avgSessionSec biased low)
instructions: File: server/src/features/admin/routes.ts
Bug 1: `leaguesOwned` currently returns `_count.ownedTeams` — that's TEAMS owned, not LEAGUES. Fix: count distinct leagues from team ownership OR use LeagueMembership with role=COMMISSIONER

Bug 2: `avgSessionSec` biased low — only counts sessions ended via endSession(), not idle-swept sessions. Fix: include sessions with status='expired' OR compute from (endedAt || lastSeenAt) - startedAt
Add unit tests for both metrics with known fixture data

tasks.title: Add requireAuth to profile endpoint (anonymous membership enumeration)
instructions: File: server/src/features/profiles/routes.ts
Current: GET /api/profiles/:userId returns membership data without auth check
Target: require auth; consider whether non-friends should see membership at all
Security-sentinel flagged this in Session 63 review as enabling user enumeration + league fingerprinting
Add test: unauthenticated request returns 401
tasks.notes: Security issue. Low effort, high value. Do this with the heartbeat fix.

tasks.title: Decide fate of unwritten UserMetrics columns (leaguesOwnedCount, leaguesCommissionedCount, lastActivityAt)
instructions: File: prisma/schema.prisma (UserMetrics model)
3 denormalized columns defined but never written anywhere: `leaguesOwnedCount`, `leaguesCommissionedCount`, `lastActivityAt`
Options: (a) wire them up in the rollup path (plan R10 promised this), (b) drop them from schema
Recommendation: drop `lastActivityAt` (redundant with lastSeenAt), wire the two league counts if cheaper than joining at read time
Schema-drift-detector flagged this as plan-fidelity gap in Session 63 review

tasks.title: Split admin/routes.ts (1031+ lines) into sub-routers
instructions: File: server/src/features/admin/routes.ts (currently 1031 lines after Session 63 additions)
Proposed split: leagueAdmin (CRUD), syncAdmin (sync-mlb, sync-stats), tasksAdmin (todos/tasks — or just todos after consolidation), usersAdmin (users list, stats), errorsAdmin (error buffer)
Keep a thin admin/routes.ts that mounts the sub-routers under their shared middleware (requireAuth + requireAdmin)
Architecture-strategist flagged this as Session 63 review finding

tasks.title: R13 impersonation — officially defer or implement behind flag
instructions: Plan at docs/plans/2026-04-13-admin-users-session-tracking-plan.md promised R13 (admin impersonation with dual-identity JWT)
Session 63 shipped without it. Review flagged as plan-fidelity gap.
Decide: (a) officially cut it with a note in the plan, (b) implement behind a feature flag
My rec: cut it. Impersonation is high-risk auditability work we don't need for MVP launch.

tasks.title: Remove Prisma `any` casts in profiles route (root cast dissolves several downstream findings)
instructions: File: server/src/features/profiles/routes.ts
kieran-typescript-reviewer: 'root cast — removing dissolves several downstream findings'
Work: find the parent cast, replace with generated Prisma types (use `generateTypes` if needed)
Follow-up: the downstream findings should auto-resolve after the root cast goes
